meaning: n.沮丧,惊慌v.使沮丧,使惊慌
examples: She let out a groan of dismay.
He glanced back, and saw to his dismay that they were gaining on him.
He looked at her in dismay.
Any Apple investor can recite these numbers in their sleep with dismay and confusion.
To my dismay, I observed that most EHRs were simply hard coding traditional clinical workflows.
